architecture.summary: SafeDec is a constrained decoding framework for autoregressive transformer-based robot navigation policies. At inference time, it intercepts the logit distributions over discrete action primitives and either masks or reweights candidate actions using formal specifications (Signal Temporal Logic) and an approximate dynamics model. An STL robustness evaluator scores each candidate action by simulating its effect on future state trajectories; this score modulates token probabilities before softmax. The method operates entirely at the decoding layer without retraining the base policy, remaining agnostic to how the policy was trained.

biblio.title: Constrained Decoding for Safe Robot Navigation Foundation Models

claims.dataset: AI2-THOR procedurally generated environments with CHORES benchmark tasks
claims.statement: HCD achieves perfect STL satisfaction with improvements ranging from 16-29 percentage points over unconstrained baselines

RCD achieves STL satisfaction improvements of 8-24 percentage points over unconstrained baselines while preserving success rates much closer to unconstrained level with drops of 0.5–6.5 percentage points
HCD reduces safety cost from 0.205 to 0.015 when combined with ISA (SafeVLA) on fragile-object and dangerous-object specifications
HCD ensures full compliance but occasionally sacrifices task success due to strict truncation; RCD's soft penalization leads to higher task success while maintaining reasonable STL satisfaction
SafeDec shows graceful degradation under significant per-step disturbances (5% translational noise, 3.3% rotational noise per step) with relatively small drops in STL satisfaction rates
Inference overhead of STL evaluation via STLCG++ is minimal at approximately 0.005 s per timestep

SafeDec assumes access to specifications defined over state space and requires localization modules for accurate state estimation, which can be difficult to design and a limiting factor for practical deployment
SafeDec requires access to an approximate dynamics model to evaluate the impact of actions on future trajectories, which can limit applicability
